questions.question: Explain the role of the transition function (δ) in a Finite-State Automaton.
expectedPoints: It defines the rules for state changes.
It takes a current state and an input symbol as arguments.
It returns the next state the automaton should enter.

questions.question: Describe the process of using a Finite-State Automaton for spelling error detection. Discuss its advantages and limitations compared to a simple dictionary lookup.
expectedPoints: An FSA is constructed to accept all correctly spelled words from a dictionary.
A word is checked by running it through the FSA; if not accepted, it is a potential error.
Advantage: Once built, checking is extremely fast (O(n) time).
Advantage: The FSA structure can be minimized to use less memory than a simple word list.
Limitation: A simple FSA can only detect errors, not suggest corrections.
Limitation: Building the initial FSA from a large dictionary requires an algorithm.
